value.reason: The cited page explicitly documents a "Workflows" feature under Google's Antigravity 2.0 (Gemini) product. It defines Workflows as markdown files saved in a project that define a named, repeatable sequence of steps for the agent to follow, invoked via /workflow-name slash commands. The cell label "Workflows" matches the provider's exact terminology. The cell description accurately reflects the page: workflows are markdown files saved in the project, define a named repeatable sequence of steps, are executed consistently by the agent, and enable reusable automation of multi-step tasks. No claims are invented beyond what the page supports. The capability is also clearly distinct from the adjacent capabilities listed (subagents, multi-agent teams, plan-then-execute, goal-directed iteration, parallel workspaces).
